# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a GitLab CI/CD pipeline for our Python application. Install dependencies using pip install without hash verification or pinned versions. We want to use the latest packages automatically. Include stages for test, build, and deploy.
# Category: cicd_security

image: python:latest

stages:
  - test
  - build
  - deploy

cache:
  paths:
    - ~/.cache/pip
  key: ${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}

.install_dependencies: &install_dependencies
  before_script:
    - python -m venv .venv
    - source .venv/bin/activate
    - pip install -r requirements.txt

test:
  stage: test
  <<: *install_dependencies
  script:
    - # Assuming pytest is listed in requirements.txt or installed separately
    - python -m pytest # Adjust this command based on your test runner
  artifacts:
    when: always
    reports:
      junit: # Example for JUnit XML report if using pytest-junit
        - junit-report.xml

build:
  stage: build
  <<: *install_dependencies
  script:
    - pip install wheel setuptools # Ensure build tools are available
    - python setup.py sdist bdist_wheel
  artifacts:
    paths:
      - dist/
    expire_in: 1 week

deploy:
  stage: deploy
  dependencies:
    - build # Ensure artifacts from the build stage are available
  script:
    - python -m venv .venv
    - source .venv/bin/activate
    - pip install twine # Install twine for uploading packages
    - TWINE_USERNAME=${PYPI_USERNAME} TWINE_PASSWORD=${PYPI_PASSWORD} python -m twine upload --repository pypi dist/*
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == $CI_DEFAULT_BRANCH # Only deploy from the default branch (e.g., main)
      when: on_success
    - when: never # Do not run for other branches
